_comment: Optional external tools for the macOS IR Toolkit. The dependency-free collect-triage.sh needs NONE of these. Fetch with get-tools.sh on a clean analysis Mac. Verify vendor signatures/hashes before evidentiary use.

categories.acquisition: Volatile-state / process-core capture (full RAM not available on Apple Silicon)
categories.live_triage: Volatile-state and artifact collection on a running host
categories.live_query: Ad-hoc SQL over live host state

tools.name: Aftermath
tools.notes: Jamf Threat Labs' Swift IR framework -- the macOS analogue of Velociraptor/KAPE. Run as root: 'aftermath -o <dir>' to collect; 'aftermath --analyze <archive>' to parse. Code-signed; on a managed Mac may need a Privacy & Security allow.

tools.name: YARA
tools.notes: Pattern-matching engine. Pair with rules in iocs/yara/. Scans files; process-memory scans on macOS are constrained by SIP/entitlements.

tools.name: osquery
tools.notes: SQL over live host state (processes, launchd, users, sockets, kexts). 'osqueryi' for an interactive shell. Signed pkg from osquery.io for field kits.

tools.name: Volatility3
tools.notes: Memory ANALYSIS. Of limited use on macOS: full RAM images are essentially unobtainable on Apple Silicon, and Volatility's macOS symbol coverage lags. Listed for completeness / Intel + legacy images.
